items.title: No guessing
items.description:
Left ⌘ = Eisuu (English) / Right ⌘ = Kana (Japanese).
CapsLock once = Eisuu / twice = Kana.

items.title: Ready in seconds
items.description:
Just grant permissions.
Pick your method from the menu bar and start typing.

items.title: Works with the latest macOS
items.description:
Built on macOS standard APIs only.
Stable and resilient to OS updates.

scroll.heading: Also: Reverse Mouse Scroll
scroll.description: macOS applies "Natural Scrolling" to both trackpad and mouse at once — there is no way to set them separately. Toggle Reverse Mouse Scroll in the menu bar to reverse only mouse wheel scrolling, while leaving trackpad as-is. Use natural scrolling on trackpad and traditional scrolling on mouse, or vice versa.

steps.title: Step 1: Download
steps.description: Click the download button above, or grab the latest EnJaSwitcher.app.zip from the GitHub Releases page.

steps.title: Step 2: Move to Applications
steps.description: Drag and drop EnJaSwitcher.app into your /Applications folder.

steps.title: Step 3: Launch & Bypass Gatekeeper
steps.description: Double-click EnJaSwitcher.app. Since this app is not notarized by Apple, macOS Gatekeeper will show a warning. Go to System Settings > Privacy & Security and click "Open Anyway". This is also required when updating to a new version. For detailed steps, see the GitHub README.

steps.title: Step 4: Grant Permissions
steps.description: On first launch, macOS will prompt you for permissions. Open "System Settings > Privacy & Security" and enable EnJaSwitcher in the following two sections.
permissions: Accessibility (required for sending virtual key events)
Input Monitoring (required for monitoring keyboard events)

permissions.heading: About Permissions
permissions.intro: This app reads keyboard events and sends virtual key presses. macOS security requires the following permissions.

items.name: Accessibility
items.reason: Required to send virtual Eisuu/Kana key press events to the system.

items.name: Input Monitoring
items.reason: Required to detect Command key and CapsLock key presses.

permissions.privacy: This app does not record or transmit your keystrokes. Permissions are managed by macOS, and the app only checks the type of key pressed (Command / CapsLock). The full source code is available on GitHub.

capslock.heading: CapsLock Mode Setup
capslock.intro: If you use CapsLock mode, you need to disable the default Caps Lock behavior in macOS to prevent conflicts.
capslock.note: Even with "No Action" set, EnJaSwitcher uses its own hardware-level monitoring, so CapsLock switching will still work correctly.

items.question: Switching doesn't work
items.answer: Check that EnJaSwitcher is enabled in both "Accessibility" and "Input Monitoring" under "System Settings > Privacy & Security". If it's already enabled but not working, remove it with the minus button and re-add it with the plus button.

items.question: "Unidentified developer" or malware warning appears
items.answer: This appears because the app is not notarized by Apple (affects all Macs). After dismissing the warning, go to System Settings > Privacy & Security and click "Open Anyway". See the GitHub README for detailed steps.

items.question: How to launch at login
items.answer: Add EnJaSwitcher in "System Settings > General > Login Items". Alternatively, you can set up a LaunchAgent (see the GitHub README for details).

items.question: How to uninstall
items.answer: Delete EnJaSwitcher.app from your Applications folder. Then remove EnJaSwitcher from the "Accessibility" and "Input Monitoring" lists in "System Settings > Privacy & Security" to clean up permission entries.

items.title: No Warranty
items.text: This app is open-source software developed by an individual. It is provided as-is with no warranty of any kind. The developer assumes no liability for any damages arising from its use.

items.title: Apple Notarization
items.text: This app is not notarized by Apple (notarization requires Apple Developer Program enrollment), so macOS Gatekeeper will block it when downloaded from the internet. This affects all Macs. Please follow the installation instructions to bypass Gatekeeper, or build from source for the most reliable experience.

items.title: Future Compatibility
items.text: This app uses the macOS CGEventTap and IOHIDManager APIs. If macOS security policies change significantly, these APIs could be affected.

items.title: Privacy
items.text: This app does not record or transmit your keystrokes. Keyboard event monitoring is used solely to detect key types (Command / CapsLock). The full source code is available on GitHub.
